originalTitle: Djibouti's president wins unprecedented sixth term with 97.8% of vote
title: Big News from Djibouti: Our President Stays!
content:
Hello from Djibouti!

Hello everyone! It's your friendly reporter here, bringing you super simple news from a wonderful country in Africa called Djibouti! Imagine a sunny place right by the big, blue sea – that's Djibouti! It's a special place with lots of kind people. Today, we have some important news about the person who helps lead this amazing country.

Meet President Guelleh

Let's talk about the leader of Djibouti. His name is Mr. Ismail Omar Guelleh. He is 78 years old. That's a lot of birthdays! President Guelleh is like the head of a very big family, but instead of just a family, he is the head of the whole country of Djibouti. Did you know? Being a president is a really big job! They work very hard to make sure their country is a good place for everyone, especially for kids like you.

What Does a President Do?

A president has many important jobs. They help make rules so everyone can live safely and fairly. They also help build schools where you can learn, and hospitals where people can get better when they are sick. Presidents also think about how to make new roads and make sure there is enough food for everyone. It's like when your teacher or your parents help decide what's best for the class or your family. A president does that for millions of people!

Where is Djibouti Anyway?

Djibouti is a small, beautiful country located in East Africa. It sits right next to the Red Sea, which is part of a very busy ocean path for big ships. Think of it like a very important stop for ships that travel all over the world! Did you know? Djibouti has a famous port where ships from many different countries come and go. It helps Djibouti trade with the rest of the world and is very important for jobs and business.

What is an Election?

Now, how do leaders like President Guelleh get to be president? Grown-ups in a country get to choose their leader in something called an 'election'. An election is like a very special choosing day. People go to special places to put a mark next to the name of the person they want to be their leader. This is called 'voting'. It's a very important way for people to say who they want to help run their country. Every single vote counts!

A Very Long Time to Lead!

President Guelleh has been the leader of Djibouti for a very, very long time. He has been president for 27 years already! That's longer than many of your parents have been alive. Wow! Recently, the people of Djibouti had another election. This was for him to start his 'sixth term'. A 'term' is like a period of time that a president leads for. So, this means he will start his sixth time leading the country. Did you know? Some countries have rules about how many times a person can be president, but in Djibouti, President Guelleh can continue to lead.

How Did People Vote This Time?

In the recent election, many grown-ups in Djibouti went to vote. And guess what? Almost all the people who voted chose President Guelleh! A huge number, nearly 98 out of every 100 people who voted, wanted him to stay as president. This is a very high number! This shows that many people in Djibouti trust him and want him to continue doing his big job.

Some People Chose Not to Vote

Now, there's another part to this story. There were some other grown-ups who wanted to be president, too. These people are sometimes called 'opposition candidates' because they have different ideas about how to lead the country. But for this election, these other groups decided not to take part. They chose not to put their names forward or ask people to vote for them. This means that President Guelleh was the main choice on the voting papers. Did you know? In many countries, there are different groups of people who all want to be leaders, and they all share their ideas so people can choose.

Why is This News Important for Everyone?

This news is very important for the people of Djibouti because it means President Guelleh will continue to lead their country. When the same person is in charge for a long time, it can mean that things stay steady and don't change very much. This can be good because people know what to expect. He will keep making choices that affect everything from the food in the markets to the books in your schools and the parks where you play. He will continue to work on plans for Djibouti's future. Did you know? Every country's leader makes decisions that can change the lives of kids and grown-ups every single day! Good leaders always try to make their country a happy, safe, and fair place for everyone.

My Friendly Farewell!

So, there you have it! President Ismail Omar Guelleh will keep being the president of Djibouti. We hope this news helps you understand a little bit more about how countries choose their leaders. Keep learning and stay curious! That's all for now from your kid-friendly reporter in Africa! Bye-bye!
summary: President Ismail Omar Guelleh will continue to lead Djibouti for a sixth time after almost all the people who voted chose him in a recent election.
whyMatters: This is important because the president makes big decisions for the country, like building schools and roads, and having the same leader for a long time can mean things might stay stable and follow the same plans for kids and families in Djibouti.

originalTitle: UK shelves Chagos deal after Trump opposition
title: A Story About Islands, Homes, and Talking!
content:
Hello, Amazing Friends!

Hi everyone! It's your kid-friendly news reporter from our wonderful continent of Africa! Today, we have a very important story from far, far away. It's about some beautiful islands and a big plan that has been paused. It's a bit like when you and your friends make a super exciting plan for playtime, but then something happens, and you have to wait a little longer. Let's learn about it together!

Meet the Chagos Islands!

Imagine a place with super blue water! So blue you can almost see the fish swimming. Imagine soft, white sandy beaches where tiny crabs play. And imagine tall, green palm trees swaying gently in the warm wind. This sounds like a dream, right? Well, there are islands just like this. They are called the Chagos Islands. Did you know? The Chagos Islands are in a very, very big ocean called the Indian Ocean. It's one of the largest oceans in the whole wide world! So many amazing sea animals call it home.

Who Lived There?

For many, many years, people lived happily on these beautiful islands. They built their homes, fished in the ocean for their food, and had their families and friends all around them. The islands were their home. Home is a very special word, isn't it? It's where you feel safe, loved, and happy with your family. Did you know? People living on islands often learn amazing ways to live with nature, like building special boats and finding yummy food from the sea!

Why Did People Leave Their Homes?

Then, something sad happened a long, long time ago. A very big country, the United Kingdom (we often call it the UK for short), was in charge of these islands. Another big country, the United States of America (let's call them the USA), wanted to build a very important airport on one of the Chagos Islands. This airport was for big planes that help keep peace and safety around the world. Because of this big airport, the people who lived there had to move. They had to leave their beautiful homes on the islands. Imagine leaving your home and all your friends! It must have been very, very hard and sad for them. They moved to other countries, but they never forgot their island homes. Did you know? Leaving your home for a new place can be a big adventure, but it's also okay to miss your old home, your friends, and your memories there.

Two Countries Talking: UK and Mauritius

So, for many years, the people who left the Chagos Islands have wanted to go back. They miss their home so much. Another country, which is quite close to the Chagos Islands, is called Mauritius. Mauritius also believes that these islands should be part of *their* country. It's a bit like two friends both wanting to play with the same toy. Because of this, the UK and Mauritius started to talk. They sat down together to try and find a way to make everyone happy. They were making a big plan, or an 'agreement', about the islands. This was a hopeful time for many people who dreamed of going back home. Did you know? Talking things out is often the best way to solve problems, even for big countries! It helps everyone understand each other.

What Was the Big Plan?

The plan was very, very important. It was about who would look after the islands. Would it be the UK, or Mauritius, or both? The plan was also about helping the people who had to leave the islands so long ago. They wanted to see if these people could finally go back home, or at least visit their old homes and feel the sand between their toes again. This was a really big step towards fairness for many.

A Different Country Steps In: The USA

But do you remember the big airport the USA built on one of the islands? It's still there! And it's still very, very important for the USA's plans to keep the world safe. When the UK and Mauritius were talking about the islands and making their big plan, the USA government had some worries. They were worried that the new plan might change things for their important airport. They thought it might make their work harder. So, the USA said they were not very happy with the plan right now. They had some strong feelings about it. It was like when you have a big group project, and one friend has a different idea that makes the plan tricky. Did you know? Military bases, like the big airport on Chagos, are special places where countries keep important planes, ships, and equipment. They are used to help keep peace and protect people around the world.

Why Did the Talks Stop?

So, what happened with the big plan? The people from the UK government said they had to stop the talks for now. They said it was like running out of time. Like when you're playing a game, and the bell rings before you finish your turn. The UK said they didn't completely forget the plan or give up on it. They just had to press the 'pause' button. The USA's worries about their important airport were a big reason why the talks paused. It meant everyone needed more time and perhaps new ideas to make a plan that worked for all three countries: the UK, Mauritius, and the USA.

What Happens Now?

This means the big plan about the Chagos Islands is not happening right now. It's on 'pause'. The people who want to go home to their beautiful islands still have to wait. That must be very disappointing for them. However, the UK government also said they are not giving up entirely. This means maybe, just maybe, they will talk again later. We hope they find a good way forward for everyone involved. Especially for the people who want to go back home to their special islands. Did you know? Even when big plans are paused, it's good to keep hoping and working towards a happy and fair ending for everyone!

Learning About Our World

This story teaches us that sometimes, even big countries have to talk and work together to solve problems. And sometimes, plans take a very, very long time. But it's always important to think about what is fair and what is right. And to remember the special feeling of home. We will keep an eye on this story from our big African home and hope for happy news for the people of Chagos! Thank you for listening, my wonderful friends!
summary: The UK government has paused talks with Mauritius about the Chagos Islands, where people want to return home, because the USA has a big airport there and had worries about the plan.
whyMatters: This matters because many people want to go back to their homes on the Chagos Islands, and this pause means they have to wait longer to return. It also shows how countries need to work together to solve big problems fairly.
